title: Polymer Cross-Linking Reaction Simulator
description: The user operates a polymer chemistry lab where the pool serves as a heated reaction vessel filled with a monomer solution, and the SpecularSilkPouf represents a polymer blob that undergoes cross-linking reactions. Users must carefully adjust temperature, catalyst concentration, pH level, and UV exposure to achieve the target cross-link density, watching the polymer visually transform as each parameter changes.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a polymer cross-linking reaction simulator. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader from a CDN. Position the pool model at the scene center as a chemical reaction vessel. Place the SpecularSilkPouf inside the pool as a polymer mass floating in solution. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 4, 6) looking at the origin with OrbitControls for inspection. Add a DirectionalLight, AmbientLight, and a PointLight beneath the pool to simulate heating glow. Create an HTML overlay panel with these interactive controls: (1) a Temperature slider from 20C to 200C that changes the pool liquid color from blue to orange-red using a ShaderMaterial or by modifying emissive properties on the pool mesh, (2) a Catalyst Concentration dropdown with options 0.1M, 0.5M, 1.0M, 2.0M that scales the polymer pouf model proportionally to simulate swelling, (3) a pH slider from 1 to 14 that adjusts the opacity and roughness of the pouf material to show denaturation effects, (4) a UV Exposure toggle button that when activated adds a purple-tinted SpotLight from above and triggers a pulsing emissive glow animation on the pouf, (5) a Stir Speed slider from 0 to 500 RPM that rotates the pouf around the Y-axis at corresponding speed, (6) a Submit Reaction button that validates all parameters against target values: Temperature 150-170C, Catalyst 1.0M, pH 6-8, UV on, Stir 200-350 RPM. Display a real-time Cross-Link Density percentage calculated from how close each parameter is to the target. On submission, validate each parameter is within range. If all pass, play a success animation where the pouf emits green particles using a simple point sprite system and the pool glows green. If any fail, highlight the failing parameter controls in red and shake the pouf model briefly. Show a floating 3D text label above the pouf displaying the current polymer state: Uncured, Partially Cured, or Fully Cured based on the density percentage.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  temperature: number,         // current reaction temperature in Celsius (20-200)
  catalystConcentration: number,         // catalyst molarity in M (0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0)
  phLevel: number,         // pH of the solution (1-14)
  uvExposureActive: boolean,         // whether UV light is currently active
  stirSpeed: number,         // rotation speed of the solution in RPM (0-100)
  polymerScale: number,         // current scale factor of the polymer blob (1.0-3.0)
  crossLinkDensity: number,         // percentage of cross-linking completion (0-100)
  reactionStability: number,         // time in seconds the reaction has been stable
  poolEmissiveIntensity: number,         // glow intensity of heated pool (0-1)
  poufOpacity: number,         // transparency of polymer material (0-1)
}
